icd10_codes.description: Multiple congenital malformations, not elsewhere classified

indications.name: Multiple Congenital Anomalies
indications.custom_value: Atrial septal defect with moderate left-to-right shunt on echocardiogram (2024-08-12), bilateral congenital hydronephrosis grade III on renal ultrasound (2024-07-20), global developmental delay — not sitting independently at 14 months, absent babbling, poor visual tracking

indications.name: Global Developmental Delay
indications.custom_value: Bayley-III assessment (2024-12-01): Cognitive composite 62 (>2 SD below mean), Motor composite 58. Not meeting any 12-month milestones at 14 months.

clinical_info.prior_testing_details: 1) Chromosomal microarray (CMA) performed 2024-09-15 at Boston Children's Hospital cytogenetics lab — NORMAL result, no pathogenic CNVs detected, no regions of homozygosity >5Mb. 2) Targeted panel for Noonan spectrum disorders (RASopathy panel, 19 genes including PTPN11, SOS1, RAF1, KRAS, BRAF) — performed 2025-01-12 at GeneDx — NEGATIVE, no pathogenic or likely pathogenic variants identified. 3) Karyotype 46,XX — normal female.
clinical_info.supplemental_notes:
Patient evaluated by Dr. Sarah Kim, MD, FACMG (board-certified Medical Geneticist, ABMGG #2015-1847) at Boston Children's Hospital Division of Genetics and Genomics on 2026-03-28. Pre-test genetic counseling completed same date by Jessica Park, MS, CGC (board-certified genetic counselor, ABGC #2018-CGC-03291). Counseling covered: test purpose, possible outcomes (positive, negative, VUS), limitations, implications for family, GINA protections. Informed consent signed and on file.

Standard clinical workup completed: echocardiogram (ASD confirmed), renal ultrasound (bilateral hydronephrosis), brain MRI (2024-10-05 — mild thinning of corpus callosum, no other structural abnormality), metabolic screen (plasma amino acids, urine organic acids, acylcarnitine profile — all normal), thyroid function — normal.

Clinical presentation does not fit a well-delineated genetic syndrome. Multi-system involvement (cardiac, renal, neurodevelopmental) with negative CMA and RASopathy panel supports WES as the most efficient diagnostic approach. Test results will directly impact medical management: positive result would guide cardiology surveillance protocol, renal follow-up frequency, developmental intervention intensity, and family recurrence risk counseling.
clinical_info.family_history: Non-consanguineous parents of East Asian descent (both Han Chinese). Maternal uncle (age 35) with mild learning difficulties — never formally evaluated. Maternal grandmother with type 2 diabetes. Paternal side unremarkable. No other family members with congenital anomalies, intellectual disability, seizures, or known genetic conditions. No history of pregnancy loss or infant death.
clinical_info.additional_info: Patient initially referred from pediatric cardiology (Dr. James Wu) after echocardiogram revealed ASD at age 2 months. Subsequent developmental concerns prompted genetics referral. Renal ultrasound obtained as part of syndromic workup revealed bilateral hydronephrosis. Patient currently receiving early intervention services (PT, OT, speech therapy 3x/week each). Trio testing requested — both parents available and consented for comparator analysis.
